title: Git Bash MSYS path conversion breaks docker exec with absolute container paths
content: Running `docker exec <container> /usr/local/bin/synap-server --health-check` from Git Bash on Windows fails with exit 127: MSYS rewrites the container-absolute path to `C:/Program Files/Git/usr/local/bin/synap-server` before docker sees it. The failure is silent inside `until` loops with output redirected — it looks like the probe never becomes healthy when in fact every exec is failing locally. Same MSYS mangling previously broke `icacls /reset` (converted `/reset` to a path). Fix: run such commands via PowerShell, or prefix with `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1`, or rely on Docker's own HEALTHCHECK status (`docker inspect --format "{{.State.Health.Status}}"`), which executes inside the container and is immune. Also validated in this task: a single-platform `docker buildx build --platform linux/arm64 --load` + the image's built-in HEALTHCHECK is a sufficient functional gate for QEMU-emulated arm64 images before publishing a multi-arch manifest (Synap arm64 boots with NEON SIMD runtime-detected; full workspace compile under QEMU took ~35 min with 5 cores).
